---
title: "Plumed Beaksedge (Rhynchospora plumosa)"
slug: "rhynchospora-plumosa"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/180830011/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Plumed Beaksedge"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/180830011/medium.jpg" alt="Plumed Beaksedge" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/damonmoore">
damonmoore</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/107508465">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=167894)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and savannas.

## Natural history notes

A grass-like wetland plant characterized by its slender stems and distinctive feathery, plume-like bristles surrounding the spikelets. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and savannas, often in sandy soils. It grows during the warmer months and contributes to the diversity and texture of wetland plant communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/167894-Rhynchospora-plumosa)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/2406)
